edges.description: Constitutive KIT D816V signaling drives ligand-independent proliferation and survival of neoplastic mast cells, producing clonal accumulation and organ infiltration.
edges.target: Clonal Mast Cell Proliferation and Organ Infiltration

edges.description: Constitutive KIT D816V signaling lowers the activation threshold of neoplastic mast cells, promoting degranulation and mediator release.
Mast cell infiltration of the liver produces hepatomegaly in advanced SM.
edges.source: Clonal Mast Cell Proliferation and Organ Infiltration

edges.description: Mast cell infiltration of the spleen produces splenomegaly in advanced SM.
edges.source: Clonal Mast Cell Proliferation and Organ Infiltration

edges.description: Organ infiltration and disease burden in advanced SM contribute to weight loss.
edges.source: Clonal Mast Cell Proliferation and Organ Infiltration

edges.description: Marrow mast cell infiltration and mediator effects on bone remodeling contribute to osteoporosis.
edges.source: Clonal Mast Cell Proliferation and Organ Infiltration

edges.description: Massive mast cell mediator release (histamine, etc.) precipitates anaphylaxis.
Vasoactive mediator release produces episodic flushing.
Histamine and other mediators produce pruritus.
Mediator effects on the GI tract produce diarrhea.
Mediator-driven gastric acid secretion and GI effects produce abdominal pain.

nodes.description: Abdominal pain from mast cell mediator release and gastrointestinal involvement.

Recurrent, often severe anaphylaxis from massive mast cell mediator release, frequently triggered by Hymenoptera venom and characteristically presenting with hypotension and syncope, sometimes without skin signs.

evidence.reference_title: Hymenoptera Anaphylaxis as a Clonal Mast Cell Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Patients with clonal mast cell disease and Hymenoptera venom anaphylaxis are commonly males, without skin lesions, and anaphylaxis is characterized by hypotension and syncope in the absence of urticaria and angioedema.

nodes.description: Constitutive KIT signaling produces a clonal proliferation of abnormal mast cells that accumulate in extracutaneous organs, classically forming multifocal dense clusters of spindled mast cells in the bone marrow. The aberrant mast cells characteristically express CD25, and infiltration of bone marrow, liver, spleen, GI tract, and bone underlies the organ damage of advanced disease.
nodes.id: Clonal Mast Cell Proliferation and Organ Infiltration

evidence.reference_title: Systemic mastocytosis in adults: 2021 Update on diagnosis, risk stratification and management.
evidence.snippet: Systemic mastocytosis (SM) results from a clonal proliferation of abnormal mast cells (MC) in extra-cutaneous organs.

evidence.reference_title: Systemic mastocytosis in adults: 2021 Update on diagnosis, risk stratification and management.
evidence.snippet: The major criterion is presence of multifocal clusters of spindled MC in the bone marrow.

nodes.description: In the great majority of adult SM patients a somatic activating KIT mutation, usually D816V in the kinase activation loop, renders the KIT receptor tyrosine kinase constitutively active and independent of its ligand stem cell factor. This sustained signaling drives downstream MAPK and PI3K/AKT pathways, conferring constitutive mast cell proliferation and survival.

evidence.reference_title: Harmonization of Diagnostic Criteria in Mastocytosis for Use in Clinical Practice: WHO vs ICC vs AIM/ECNM.
evidence.snippet: In most patients with SM, a somatic KIT mutation, usually D816V, is identified.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and Safety of Midostaurin in Advanced Systemic Mastocytosis.
evidence.snippet: The multikinase inhibitor midostaurin inhibits KIT D816V, a primary driver of disease pathogenesis.

nodes.description: Diarrhea and other gastrointestinal symptoms from mast cell mediators and GI infiltration.
Episodic flushing from histamine and other vasoactive mediator release.
Hepatomegaly, sometimes with hepatic dysfunction or ascites, from mast cell organ infiltration in advanced SM (a "C" finding).

evidence.reference_title: Detection of the KIT D816V mutation in peripheral blood of systemic mastocytosis: diagnostic implications.
evidence.snippet: Recent studies have found the KIT D816V mutation in peripheral blood of virtually all adult systemic mastocytosis patients once highly sensitive PCR techniques were used

evidence.reference_title: Harmonization of Diagnostic Criteria in Mastocytosis for Use in Clinical Practice: WHO vs ICC vs AIM/ECNM.
evidence.snippet: In most patients with SM, a somatic KIT mutation, usually D816V, is identified.

nodes.description: Activated neoplastic mast cells degranulate and release preformed and newly synthesized mediators (histamine, tryptase, prostaglandins, leukotrienes, cytokines). Mediator release drives the symptomatic manifestations of SM, including anaphylaxis, flushing, pruritus, hypotension, and gastrointestinal symptoms, and is a primary management target in indolent disease.

evidence.reference_title: Systemic mastocytosis in adults: 2021 Update on diagnosis, risk stratification and management.
evidence.snippet: Treatment goals for ISM patients are primarily directed towards anaphylaxis prevention/symptom control/osteoporosis treatment.

nodes.description: Osteoporosis is a frequent and treatment-relevant skeletal complication of indolent SM, attributed to mast-cell-derived mediators affecting bone remodeling.

evidence.reference_title: Systemic mastocytosis in adults: 2021 Update on diagnosis, risk stratification and management.
evidence.snippet: Treatment goals for ISM patients are primarily directed towards anaphylaxis prevention/symptom control/osteoporosis treatment.

nodes.description: Generalized pruritus driven by mast cell mediator release.
Splenomegaly, sometimes with hypersplenism, from mast cell infiltration in advanced SM.
Syncope from mediator-induced hypotension, a frequent feature of SM-associated anaphylaxis.

evidence.reference_title: Hymenoptera Anaphylaxis as a Clonal Mast Cell Disorder.
evidence.snippet: anaphylaxis is characterized by hypotension and syncope in the absence of urticaria and angioedema

nodes.description: Weight loss from malabsorption and mast cell GI infiltration, a "C" finding of advanced SM.
